---
title: "Structural Causal Architecture of Macroeconomics"
author: "Quantitative Researcher"
date: "July 2026"
output: html_document
---

## Executive Summary
Standard regression models suffer from severe endogeneity and omitted variable bias when analyzing macroeconomic systems. To isolate true structural impacts, this research utilizes constraint-based structure learning to discover the causal topology between Yields, Inflation proxies, and the Nifty Bank Index.

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(quantmod)
library(bnlearn)
library(knitr)

set.seed(100)

# 1. Fetch Daily Bank Index (Yahoo Finance)
suppressWarnings(getSymbols("^NSEBANK", src = "yahoo", from = "2015-01-01", to = Sys.Date()))

# 2. Fetch Real Macro Proxies (Yahoo Finance - NO API KEY REQUIRED)
# ^TNX = US 10-Year Yield (Global Yield Benchmark)
# GLD = SPDR Gold Trust (Classic Inflation Proxy)
suppressWarnings(getSymbols(c("^TNX", "GLD"), src = "yahoo", from = "2015-01-01", to = Sys.Date()))

# 3. Merge and Clean 
merged_data <- merge(Ad(NSEBANK), Ad(TNX), Ad(GLD))
merged_data <- na.omit(merged_data) 

# 4. Format for the Causal Engine
causal_df <- data.frame(merged_data)
colnames(causal_df) <- c("Bank", "Yield", "Inflation_Proxy")
dag <- bnlearn::hc(causal_df)
plot(dag, main = "Macroeconomic Causal Network")
strength <- bnlearn::boot.strength(causal_df, R = 100, algorithm = "hc")
top_relationships <- head(strength[order(-strength$strength), ])
kable(top_relationships, row.names = FALSE, digits = 3)
